samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal indicating the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="i">Shift count register to control the multiplication operation.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="i" initial_value="0">Shift count register initialized to 0.</reg>
        <always id="i" sensitivity="posedge clk or negedge rst_n">
            <if id="i" condition="rst_n == 0">
                <assignment>Set the shift count register (i) to 0.</assignment>
            </if>
            <else id="i" condition="start == 1 and i < 17">
                <assignment>Increment the shift count register (i) by 1.</assignment>
            </else>
            <else id="i" condition="start == 0">
                <assignment>Reset the shift count register (i) to 0.</assignment>
            </else>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="done_r" initial_value="0">Multiplication completion flag initialized to 0.</reg>
        <always id="done_r" sensitivity="posedge clk or negedge rst_n">
            <if id="done_r" condition="rst_n == 0">
                <assignment>Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0.</assignment>
            </if>
            <else id="done_r" condition="i == 16">
                <assignment>Set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1.</assignment>
            </else>
            <else id="done_r" condition="i == 17">
                <assignment>Reset the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0.</assignment>
            </else>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <reg id="areg" initial_value="[15:0]0">Multiplicand register initialized to 0.</reg>
        <reg id="breg" initial_value="[15:0]0">Multiplier register initialized to 0.</reg>
        <reg id="yout_r" initial_value="[31:0]0">Product register initialized to 0.</reg>
        <always id="yout_r" sensitivity="posedge clk or negedge rst_n">
            <if id="yout_r" condition="rst_n == 0">
                <assignment>Reset the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0.</assignment>
            </if>
            <else id="yout_r" condition="start == 1">
                <if id="yout_r" condition="i == 0">
                    <assignment>Store the input multiplicand (ain) in the multiplicand register (areg) and the input multiplier (bin) in the multiplier register (breg).</assignment>
                </if>
                <else id="yout_r" condition="i > 0 and i < 17">
                    <if id="yout_r" condition="areg[i-1] == 1">
                        <assignment>Accumulate the product by shifting the multiplier register (breg) left by i-1 positions and appending 0 bits at the least significant bit positions.</assignment>
                    </if>
                </else>
            </else>
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module performs unsigned 16-bit multiplication using shift and accumulate operations. 
    It maintains a shift count (i) to control the multiplication process and generates a completion flag (done) to indicate when the operation is finished. 
    On each clock cycle, if the reset signal is active, the shift count is reset to 0. 
    If the start signal is asserted, the module begins the multiplication process by storing the input multiplicand and multiplier in internal registers. 
    For each shift count greater than 0 and less than 17, if the corresponding bit in the multiplicand register is set, the module accumulates the shifted multiplier in the product register. 
    When the shift count reaches 16, the multiplication operation is complete, and the done flag is set to 1.
    </implementation>
</module>
